evals.prompt: Create a WER-EA dosage-window figure package with simulated template data. Assume matplotlib or another required Python plotting package is not installed locally.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must follow the Python-only figure package contract. It should report the missing Python plotting dependency, provide or offer a Python-only script and install/run instructions, and stop before rendering.

assertions.name: no_alternate_backend_rendering
assertions.description: When Python plotting dependencies are unavailable, no alternate plotting backend preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is generated as a substitute.

assertions.name: python_blocker_reported
assertions.description: The response clearly reports the missing Python runtime or package blocker and does not present the figure package as completed output.

evals.prompt: Make a journal-ready WER-EA mechanism-map figure package from the provided source table and caption notes.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should follow the Python-only figure package contract and produce or specify a complete figure package with figure_contract.md, source_data.csv or source_map.json, plot.py, SVG/PDF/PNG/TIFF exports, caption.md, qa_report.md, and asset_manifest.md. Visual QA must pass before claiming the figure package is journal-ready.

assertions.name: complete_figure_package
assertions.description: The figure package includes contract, source data or source map, Python plotting script, export bundle, caption, QA report, and asset manifest.

assertions.name: visual_qa_before_ready_claim
assertions.description: The assistant does not call the figure package ready unless visual QA has passed.

evals.prompt: Given source CSV data for multiple materials experiments, create publication-ready Python figures for scatter regression, boxplot with points, violin distribution, contour response map, 3D response surface, polar performance, errorbar trend, dual-axis trend, correlation heatmap, and stacked composition.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should route all ten chart families through the Python-only plotting helpers, produce SVG and PNG outputs for each figure when dependencies are available, print or draft reviewer-safe captions, and keep claims bounded by the source data.

assertions.name: all_chart_families_supported
assertions.description: The figure package or script gallery covers the ten expanded Python chart families.

assertions.name: figure_package
assertions.description: Each generated figure package includes source data, Python plotting code, SVG/PNG exports, and a caption boundary.

evals.prompt: Create a multi-panel XRD overlay figure package with simulated phase-identification data. Assume Python itself is not installed locally (no python or python3 command available).
evals.expected_output: The assistant must not use any alternate plotting backend or hand-drawn substitute to draw a preview or export. It should report that the Python runtime is unavailable, provide or offer a Python-only script and install instructions, and stop before rendering.

assertions.name: no_alternate_backend_rendering
assertions.description: When the Python runtime is unavailable, no alternate backend preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is generated as a substitute.

assertions.name: python_runtime_blocker_reported
assertions.description: The response clearly reports that Python itself is missing and does not present a figure as completed output.

evals.prompt: Make a publication-ready figure from some materials data. Do not provide source data, a figure contract, caption claims, or evidence anchors — just ask for a nice figure.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must not generate mock data, write plotting scripts, create previews, or render placeholder figures. It should stop and ask for the claim, source-data anchor, and figure contract before any rendering, per the blocking gate in the backend axis detect block.

assertions.name: no_mock_data_generated
assertions.description: No mock, simulated, or placeholder data is generated when the user has not provided source data.

assertions.name: no_rendering_without_contract
assertions.description: No plotting script, preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is produced before the claim, source-data anchor, and figure contract are established.

assertions.name: contract_gate_request_made
assertions.description: The response explicitly asks for the claim, source-data anchor, and/or figure contract before proceeding to render.
